title: aethionema saxatile subsp. scopulorum
intro: Aethionema saxatile subsp. scopulorum, also known as Saxatile Rock Cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and a compact, shrubby growth habit. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aethionema saxatile subsp. scopulorum are said to include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, and can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
